title: Add streaming Codex review detail flow
what_changed: The backend now introduces a reusable repo task runner and in-memory log bus so Codex summaries can stream progress, write JSON results to a mounted output directory, and expose diff and log SSE endpoints for each change. Summary generation is refactored to pass base and head refs through a dedicated prompt builder/validator path, and the worker/state machine now support rerunning summarization from `ready_for_review`. The change detail UI is expanded with annotated summaries, live Codex log streaming, raw diff and file tree viewers, header injection, and a regenerate-summary action wired to new API helpers. Dependency and styling updates add Pierre diff/tree packages, Preact runtime support, and a new global theme stylesheet to support the richer review experience.
risk_assessment: Main risk is in the new log-stream lifecycle: `/api/changes/:id/logs` returns `done` immediately once a change leaves `summarizing`, which can bypass the buffered replay behavior that `LogBus` is designed to provide for late subscribers. The Docker runner also now relies on either a pinned Codex CLI plus mounted host `~/.codex` auth or an API key, so environment mismatches could break summary generation outside the primary dev setup.

annotations.text: The backend now introduces a reusable repo task runner and in-memory log bus so Codex summaries can stream progress, write JSON results to a mounted output directory, and expose diff and log SSE endpoints for each change.

The change detail UI is expanded with annotated summaries, live Codex log streaming, raw diff and file tree viewers, header injection, and a regenerate-summary action wired to new API helpers.
Dependency and styling updates add Pierre diff/tree packages, Preact runtime support, and a new global theme stylesheet to support the richer review experience.
